mission.title: argo-workflows: 3.5 ListWorkflows causes server to hang when there are lots of archived workflows
mission.description: 3.5 ListWorkflows causes server to hang when there are lots of archived workflows. Requested by 45+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-workflows deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-workflows version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
helm list -n argo-workflows 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-workflows installation.

steps.title: Review argo-workflows configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-workflows configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get configmap -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-workflows
```
With over 200,000 rows in the workflow archive table, the ListWorkflows API causes the argo-server to hang because it tries to load all archived workflows into memory at once.

steps.title: Apply the fix for 3.5 ListWorkflows causes server to hang when there are lots…
steps.description:
This reverts #11761. See https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/issues/12025 for context.
```yaml
orderBy = ...
filters = ...
limit = 20
cursor = 0, 0
k8sResults = fetchK8s(cursor[0], limit, filters, orderBy)
dbResults = fetchDB(cursor[1], limit, filters, orderBy)
results = mergeResults(k8sResults, dbResults).slice(0, limit)

newK8sOffset = getLastK8sResult(results)
newDBOffset = getLastDbResult(results)
newCursor = (newK8sOffset, newDBOffset)
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get events -n argo-workflows --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "3.5 ListWorkflows causes server to hang when there are lots…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Reverted the problematic commit (#11761) that caused ListWorkflows to load all archived workflows into memory. Implemented a dual-cursor pagination approach that merges Kubernetes and database results with proper offsets.
codeSnippets:
orderBy = ...
filters = ...
limit = 20
cursor = 0, 0
k8sResults = fetchK8s(cursor[0], limit, filters, orderBy)
dbResults = fetchDB(cursor[1], limit, filters, orderBy)
results = mergeResults(k8sResults, dbResults).slice(0, limit)

newK8sOffset = getLastK8sResult(results)
newDBOffset = getLastDbResult(results)
newCursor = (newK8sOffset, newDBOffset)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
